claim: A domain's vendored scenario-recorder, when present at <domainDir>/spec/hotamspec/hotamspec.go, MUST be byte-identical (post-banner) to the engine's own canonical recorder source (internal/recorder/canon), so that every domain running the scenario API runs the SAME contract the engine itself defines and generates -- never a stale, locally-patched, or divergent copy. check_recorder_current (internal/invariants/recorder_check.go) mechanically enforces this via a sha256 comparison between the vendored file and the engine canon, and is an honest no-op for a domain that has not yet vendored a recorder (no file at that path).
why: PLAN-scenario-generated-spec.md §2 D1: the scenario-recorder (the hotamspec Scenario/Given/When/Then API) is VENDORED into a consumer domain's own spec/ module as a generated single-package file -- the same crystal-style copy pattern, NOT a cross-module `replace` (which would reopen the NEW-2-bis dependency debt). Vendoring means the domain compiles and runs its OWN copy; without an integrity check that copy could silently drift from the engine canon (a partial update, a local patch, a stale vendoring), so a domain's scenario artifacts would be produced by a DIFFERENT contract than the engine documents and generates SPEC.md from -- defeating the byte-identical-across-domains guarantee the recorder's deterministic renderer exists to provide. check_recorder_current closes that the same filesystem-aware, honest-no-op-when-absent shape check_graph_lock_pins_graph_json already established for graph.json+graph.lock integrity (R-no-hand-edit-graph): a vendored recorder that diverges from the canon is the recorder-side analog of a hand-edited graph, and is detected the same way (sha256, content-addressed). This requirement is the self-hosting anchor (W3.2 spirit) for the orphan-enforcer gate check_bijection_r_to_enforcer flagged on hotam-spec-self: the engine holds its own vendored-recorder integrity obligation, symmetric to the one it imposes on consumer domains.
evidence: PLAN-scenario-generated-spec.md §2 D1 and §3 W1.1 name check_recorder_current verbatim and the vendoring+sha256 shape; internal/invariants/recorder_check.go registers the check_* with its full Rule/Why; this anchor closes the W3.2 self-hosting debt where check_bijection_r_to_enforcer flagged check_recorder_current as an orphan enforcer on hotam-spec-self
